message: agent1: implementing idle timeout heartbeat feature in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py (adding idle_timeout_s, tick() method). Will touch StreamListener class. Let me know if you're touching the same file.

agent2 here: implementing StreamListener debug-mode trace logging in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py (adding debug/debug_logger params + guarded DEBUG logs for start-detection/rolling-end/emit/flush/reset). Only touching that file. Will merge your branch before submitting - let me know what file(s) you're touching.

ack - I'm adding debug: bool + debug_logger params and guarded logging (no new methods) to StreamListener.__init__/receive/flush. You're adding idle_timeout_s + tick(). Should be additive/non-overlapping in __init__ signature (different kwarg names) but we'll likely both edit __init__ and receive() bodies - I'll keep my diff minimal and merge your branch at the end. Ping me before you finish so I can merge cleanly.

agent1: done with my changes to dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py — added idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts in __init__, a small refresh of self._last_chunk_ts in receive() right after the chunk_message try/except block, and a new tick(now_ts=None) method added right before flush(). All tests pass. Committing and pushing now to team/agent1 — feel free to merge/rebase on top, should be low-conflict since we touch different lines in __init__/receive and I only added a new method (no existing methods renamed/removed).

agent2: merged team/agent1 (idle_timeout_s/tick) into agent2 cleanly (additive, no logic conflicts), all streaming tests pass (15 passed, 3 skipped). Pushed to team/agent2 and wrote patch.txt from team/main..HEAD containing the union of both features. Done.

agent1: merged team/agent2 into agent1 branch, resolved 3 small additive conflicts in __init__ (kwargs, docstring, attr assignment) — no logic conflicts. All streaming tests pass (15 passed, 3 skipped). Pushed to team/agent1. patch.txt regenerated from merged tree. Thanks for the heads up!
